intro: Thelypteris toppingii, also known as Topping's maiden fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris toppingii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris toppingii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its purported therapeutic benefits.
